what: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: table_regression_models() returns registry model classes supported table_regression(), one row per engine, class's family, average-marginal-effects estimand, exponentiate semantics, labelled table blocks. registry drives page's table, published list drift code. page also reference per-family behaviour (sections ). reachable ?table_regression_models, ?table_regression_mixed, ?table_regression_ordinal, ?table_regression_counts, ?table_regression_categorical, ?table_regression_survival, ?table_regression_robust, ?table_regression_bayesian. class listed: fit model call table_regression(fit) anyway – unsupported classes error clear message naming supported set. Feature requests welcome issue tracker.

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
code: table_regression_models()

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: data frame one row per supported engine columns family, class, engine, ame, exponentiate, blocks.

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: robust vcov request honoured class's field-standard backend, refused clear error naming supported set; footer always names estimator actually applied. exponentiate = TRUE link-gated: produces labelled ratio (/ IRR / HR / RR / MR / TR) link warrants one. Identity-link fits warn left untouched; non-ratio links (probit, cauchit, inverse, ...) refused clear error. Class-specific structure renders labelled subordinate blocks rows table, explained footer line. Fit statistics default family's field standard (show_fit_stats overrides; class-inappropriate tokens rejected pointer right ones). Everything available programmatically: broom::tidy(), glance(), as_structured(), .data.frame().

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: Fixed effects: Satterthwaite t (lmer + lmerTest), Wald z (glmer, glmmTMB), containment-df t (lme). Random effects render Random effects block rows (SD / correlation / residual SE CI; re_scale, re_columns), deliberately per-row p-value (boundary-invalid Wald; Self & Liang 1987) – footer carries chi-bar-squared LR test whole random part, re_test = "lrt" / "rlrt" adds opt-boundary-correct per-term test. N (groups) ICC fit-stat rows; Nakagawa marginal / conditional R-squared default R-squared family. CR* robust via clubSandwich (glmmTMB: conditional part , disclosed).

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: Cut-points render Thresholds block (log-odds scale, never exponentiated; show_thresholds). Partial-proportional-odds clm terms render Non-proportional effects block, one coefficient per cut-point. exponentiate yields proportional odds ratios logit; ci_method = "profile" profiles predictor coefficients. AME per-category (marginal effect P(Y = k)). Defaults include McFadden Nagelkerke pseudo-R-squared. See vignette("table-regression-ordinal").

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: Two-part models show full model: zero component renders Zero-inflation block (zeroinfl, glmmTMB ziformula: probability structural zero) Zero hurdle block (hurdle: probability nonzero count – opposite direction, hence distinct label), Dispersion block dispformula covariates. Component coefficients join p_adjust family take stars; zero component exponentiated logit link (odds ratio). AME combined-response effect E(Y). CR* pscl fits covers components via sandwich::vcovCL(). Opt show_components = FALSE.

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: multinom renders per non-reference outcome; exponentiate yields odds ratios outcome reference outcome – baseline-category logits log-odds (Agresti; SAS prints "Odds Ratio Estimates" generalized-logit link; Stata's mlogit, rrr labels quantity relative-risk ratio). AME per-outcome. nested = TRUE compares nested multinom fits likelihood-ratio test (anova.multinom() convention). mlogit renders per-alternative rows; AME refused (slopes() method exists data format). CR* available one cluster value per choice situation, n counts choice situations; HC* refused (sandwich::vcovHC() mis-scales meat mlogit's per-chooser score structure).

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: Cox models exponentiate hazard ratios; survreg log-scale distributions time ratios (identity-scale distributions left untouched). AME refused Cox fits (marginal-probability effect hazard scale); absolute-effect columns "rmst" "risk_diff" families instead – covariate-adjusted RMST cumulative-incidence differences g-computation, mandatory tau / at_time horizons (right-censored single-record coxph fits without strata() tt()). CR* uses Lin-Wei grouped-dfbeta sandwich (coxph) rms::robcov() (cph, needs x = TRUE, y = TRUE). nested = TRUE compares nested Cox fits likelihood-ratio test.

what: Robust, IV, quantile and panel models
title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: estimatr fits keep robust SEs (never overwritten); quantreg::rq() honours se = method, including rank-inversion CIs; fixest fits report fixed-effects structure footer.

title: Supported models and per-family behaviour of table_regression() — table_regression_models
text: Posterior median, posterior SD, equal-tailed credible intervals; deliberately p-value column stars.

what: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: Fits one model per candidate predictor (univariable screen) renders single table one row block per predictor. multivariable = TRUE (default), full model containing predictors merged side side "Univariable" / "Multivariable" column groups – standard presentation applied epidemiology (gtsummary::tbl_uvregression() + tbl_merge() workflow).

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
code: table_regression_uv(   data,   outcome,   predictors,   method = c("glm", "lm", "coxph"),   family = stats::binomial(),   multivariable = TRUE,   complete_cases = FALSE,   show_columns = c("n", "b", "ci", "p"),   title = NULL,   ... )

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: data data frame. outcome outcome column (unquoted name, tidyselect). method = "coxph", Surv(time, status) expression evaluated data (tbl_uvregression convention). predictors Candidate predictor columns (tidyselect, e.g. c(age, sex, education) (.numeric)). outcome column(s) dropped selection automatically. method "glm" (default), "lm", "coxph" (requires survival package; estimates render HRs exponentiate = TRUE). family stats::family object method = "glm". Default binomial(). Refused method = "coxph", gaussian() identity link refused : use method = "lm" linear screen. multivariable Logical, default TRUE: merge full model (predictors together) second column group. complete_cases Logical, default FALSE. TRUE restricts models rows complete outcome + every predictor (common-sample comparison); reduction disclosed table note. show_columns Passed table_regression(). Default c("n", "b", "ci", "p") – tbl_uvregression column set; "n" per-predictor sample size. multivariable group carries N column (single n fit-statistics row, reference layouts). binary outcomes, add "n_events" outcome event counts events/N per factor level (column group counts estimation sample). title Table title; NULL (default) builds "Univariable multivariable <type> regression: <outcome>". ... Passed table_regression() (exponentiate, vcov, cluster, p_adjust, digits, labels, output, ...). show_intercept defaults FALSE ; nested meaningful screen refused. cluster must single vector one value per row data; aligned fit's estimation sample automatically.

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: See table_regression() (output contract).

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: default univariable model fit complete cases, N varies across predictors – N column discloses (shown first row block), table note states whenever Ns differ. multivariable model fit complete cases variables (n appears fit-statistics rows). Pass complete_cases = TRUE restrict every model – univariable multivariable – common complete-case sample.

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: p_adjust (passed table_regression()) treats whole univariable screen ONE family (screened coefficients together); multivariable model family, multi-model table.

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: Hidden default sides (univariable fit nuisance intercept), matching gtsummary::tbl_regression()'s intercept = FALSE default. Pass show_intercept = TRUE display .

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
text: Batra, N. et al. (Eds.) (2021). Epidemiologist R Handbook, Univariate multivariable regression. https://epirhandbook.com/en/new_pages/regression.html Sjoberg, D.D., Whiting, K., Curry, M., Lavery, J.., & Larmarange, J. (2021). Reproducible summary tables gtsummary package. R Journal, 13(1), 570-580.

title: Univariable screening table (with optional multivariable merge) — table_regression_uv
code: # \donttest{ table_regression_uv(   sochealth,   outcome    = smoking,   predictors = c(age, sex, education),   family     = binomial(),   exponentiate = TRUE ) #> Ordered factor(s) detected. Polynomial contrasts (the R default for `ordered()`) decompose the factor into orthogonal trend components: `.L` = linear, `.Q` = quadratic, `.C` = cubic, `^k` = degree k. title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: uncertainty_coef() computes Uncertainty Coefficient (Theil's U) two-way contingency table, based information entropy.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
code: uncertainty_coef(   x,   direction = c("symmetric", "row", "column"),   detail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95,   digits = 3L,   .include_se = FALSE )

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: x contingency table (class table). direction Direction prediction: "symmetric" (default), "row" (column predicts row), "column" (row predicts column). detail Logical. FALSE (default), return estimate numeric scalar. TRUE, return named numeric vector including confidence interval p-value. conf_level number 0 1 giving confidence level (default 0.95). used detail = TRUE. Set NULL omit confidence interval. digits Number decimal places used printing result (default 3). affects detail = TRUE output. .include_se Internal parameter; use.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: structure cramer_v(): scalar detail = FALSE, named vector detail = TRUE. p-value tests H0: U = 0 (Wald z-test).

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
text: uncertainty coefficient measures association using Shannon entropy. Let \(H_X\) \(H_Y\) marginal entropies row column variables respectively, \(H_{XY}\) joint entropy. direction = "row" (column predicts row): \(U = (H_X + H_Y - H_{XY}) / H_X\). direction = "column" (row predicts column): \(U = (H_X + H_Y - H_{XY}) / H_Y\). direction = "symmetric": \(U = 2 (H_X + H_Y - H_{XY}) / (H_X + H_Y)\). entropy terms use standard mathematical convention \(0 \log 0 = 0\), matching SPSS / PSPP CROSSTABS definition Cover & Thomas (2006). Note DescTools::UncertCoef() applies additional Laplace correction (replacing zero cells \(1/n^2\)) entropy computation, produces slightly different point estimates tables empty cells; correction uncommon information-theory literature used . asymptotic standard errors follow DescTools delta method; see cramer_v() full references.

title: Uncertainty Coefficient — uncertainty_coef
code: tab <- table(sochealth$smoking, sochealth$education) uncertainty_coef(tab) #> [1] 0.01148762 uncertainty_coef(tab, direction = "row", detail = TRUE) #> Estimate  CI lower  CI upper     p #>    0.018     0.003     0.032  .021

what: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
text: varlist() lists variables data frame extracts essential metadata: variable names, labels, summary values, classes, number distinct values, number valid (non-missing) observations, number missing values. Tidyselect-style selectors can supplied pick reorder columns dynamically. vl() convenient shorthand varlist() offers identical functionality shorter name.

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
code: varlist(   x,   ...,   values = FALSE,   tbl = FALSE,   include_na = FALSE,   factor_levels = c("observed", "all") )  vl(   x,   ...,   values = FALSE,   tbl = FALSE,   include_na = FALSE,   factor_levels = c("observed", "all") )

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
text: x data frame, transformation one. ... Optional tidyselect-style column selectors (e.g. starts_with("var"), (.numeric), etc.). Columns can selected reordered, renaming selections supported. values Logical. FALSE (default), displays compact summary variable's values. numeric, character, date/time, labelled, factor variables, unique non-missing values shown four; otherwise first three values, ellipsis (...), last value shown. Values sorted appropriate (e.g., numeric, character, date). factors, factor_levels controls whether observed declared levels shown; level order preserved. labelled variables, prefixed labels displayed via labelled::to_factor(levels = "prefixed"). TRUE, unique non-missing values displayed. tbl Logical. FALSE (default), opens summary Viewer session interactive. TRUE, returns tibble. include_na Logical. TRUE, unique missing value markers (<NA>, <NaN>) explicitly appended end Values summary present variable. applies variable types. Literal strings "NA", "NaN", "" quoted distinguish missing markers. FALSE (default), missing values omitted Values still counted NAs column. factor_levels Character. Controls factor values displayed Values. "observed" (default; code_book() uses "") shows levels present data, preserving factor level order. "" shows declared levels, including unused levels.

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
text: tibble one row per selected variable, containing following columns: Variable: variable names Label: variable labels (available via label attribute) Values: summary variable's values, depending values include_na arguments. values = FALSE, compact summary shown: unique values four, otherwise 3 + ... + last. values = TRUE, unique non-missing values displayed. labelled variables, prefixed labels displayed using labelled::to_factor(levels = "prefixed"). factors, levels displayed according factor_levels. Matrix array columns summarized dimensions. Missing value markers (<NA>, <NaN>) optionally appended end (controlled via include_na). Literal strings "NA", "NaN", "" quoted distinguish missing markers. Class: class variable (possibly multiple, e.g. "labelled", "numeric") N_distinct: number distinct non-missing values N_valid: number non-missing observations NAs: number missing observations matrix array columns, observations counted per row: row treated missing cells NA. N_valid / NAs therefore count complete vs. incomplete rows, individual cells. tbl = FALSE (default) tibble sent Viewer (interactive) surfaced via message (non-interactive) function returns invisibly NULL. Set tbl = TRUE return tibble directly downstream use.

title: Generate a comprehensive summary of the variables — varlist
text: interactive session (RStudio, Positron, ...), summary opens Viewer pane contextual title like vl: sochealth. data frame transformed subsetted, title suffixed * (e.g. vl: sochealth*); anonymous ambiguous calls fall back vl: <data>. Pass tbl = TRUE return tibble instead. default factor_levels = "observed" mirrors actually data; code_book() defaults "" document declared schema. See @param factor_levels override either default.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: yule_q() computes Yule's Q coefficient association 2x2 contingency table.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
code: yule_q(x, detail = FALSE, conf_level = 0.95, digits = 3L, .include_se = FALSE)

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: x contingency table (class table). detail Logical. FALSE (default), return estimate numeric scalar. TRUE, return named numeric vector including confidence interval p-value. conf_level number 0 1 giving confidence level (default 0.95). used detail = TRUE. Set NULL omit confidence interval. digits Number decimal places used printing result (default 3). affects detail = TRUE output. .include_se Internal parameter; use.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: structure cramer_v(): scalar detail = FALSE, named vector detail = TRUE. p-value tests H0: Q = 0 (Wald z-test).

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
text: 2x2 table cells \(, b, c, d\), Yule's Q \(Q = (ad - bc) / (ad + bc)\). equivalent Goodman-Kruskal Gamma 2x2 tables. asymptotic standard error \(SE = 0.5 (1 - Q^2) \sqrt{1/+ 1/b + 1/c + 1/d}\). Edge cases: ad + bc = 0, Q undefined function returns NA spicy_undefined_stat warning. cell zero (ad + bc > 0), Q well-defined SE formula divides zero – point estimate returned, se, ci_lower, ci_upper, p_value NA. Standard error formulas follow DescTools implementations (Signorell et al., 2024); see cramer_v() full references.

title: Yule's Q — yule_q
code: tab <- table(sochealth$smoking, sochealth$sex) yule_q(tab) #> [1] -0.02632653

title: spicy (development version)
text: table_regression(exponentiate = TRUE) now errors links whose exponentiated coefficient ratio (probit, cauchit, inverse, sqrt, …). Ratio links (logit, log, binomial / ordinal cloglog) unchanged; identity links keep warn--skip. table_categorical() defaults drop_na = FALSE: missing values display "(Missing)" level instead silently removed. drop_na = TRUE, table note now reports removed. standardized = "smart" scales continuous inputs 2 SD leaves binary inputs (0/1 factor dummies) unscaled, Gelman (2008) defines . rule applied inverted since 0.12.0, halving every continuous “smart” beta. align = "auto" removed table_*() functions; use "decimal" (default), "center", "right". table_regression(show_fit_stats = character(0)) errors; use FALSE suppress block. Multi-model show_columns = "all_b" / "all_ame" auto-compact (CIs dropped); request atomic tokens keep . tidy() labels AME rows estimate_type = "ame" ("AME"). SE footer reads "classical (Fisher information)" ("classical (MLE inverse Hessian)").

title: spicy (development version)
text: table_regression() gains support 30 model classes beyond lm / glm. See ?table_regression_models full registry per-family behaviour, new vignettes walk-throughs. Mixed effects (lme4::lmer / glmer, glmmTMB, nlme::lme): random effects block rows (SD, correlations, residual, SE CI), ICC, per-group N marginal / conditional R² fit statistics, boundary-corrected LR test model without random effects. test follows fit’s estimator (REML ML) full specification (prior weights, variance correlation structures, zero-inflation). Bayesian (rstanarm, brms): posterior median, SD, equal-tailed credible intervals; p-values. Survival (survival::coxph / survreg, rms::cph, flexsurv::flexsurvreg): Cox tables report n N events fit statistics concordance footer note. Categorical ordinal (nnet::multinom, mlogit::mlogit, MASS::polr, ordinal::clm): single multinom renders outcome categories column groups (outcome_labels relabels ); mlogit uses two-segment alternative-specific layout; ordinal thresholds render labelled block (show_thresholds = FALSE opt ), partial-proportional-odds clm fits render non-proportional terms block. Survey (survey::svyglm); robust / IV / panel (estimatr, AER::ivreg, fixest); beta, Tobit, two-part counts (betareg, AER::tobit, pscl::zeroinfl / hurdle); plus MASS::rlm / glm.nb, quantreg::rq, mgcv::gam / bam, stats::nls, rms::ols / lrm / Glm, sampleSelection::selection. Requests class honour refused classed error (spicy_unsupported_vcov, spicy_unsupported_standardized) instead rendering empty column.

title: spicy (development version)
text: table_regression_uv(): univariable screening tables. One fit per candidate predictor, rendered one table row block per predictor merged side side multivariable model. Supports glm (default), lm, coxph (outcome = Surv(time, status)). per-predictor N column shown default note discloses Ns differ across fits; complete_cases = TRUE forces common sample. Intercepts hidden default p_adjust treats whole screen one family. exponentiate, vcov / cluster (footer names cluster column), labels, output engines, tidy() work table_regression(). table_regression_models(): machine-readable registry supported model classes (family, engine, AME, exponentiate semantics); help page per-family reference.

title: spicy (development version)
text: New show_columns token families "rmst" "risk_diff" coxph fits: covariate-adjusted differences restricted mean survival time [0, tau] cumulative incidence at_time, computed g-computation fitted model, bootstrap SEs, CIs, p-values. Factor predictors get one row per level (vs reference); continuous predictors report +1-unit contrast. horizon explicit required – tau = "minmax" picks smallest per-group maximum follow-– table note states estimand replicate count. New show_columns token "n_events": outcome event counts displayed events/N next estimates – per factor level (reference row included) model totals continuous rows, computed model’s estimation sample. Available binomial outcomes – glm, glmer, glmmTMB fits alike – right-censored coxph fits; opt-everywhere. re_ci = "profile": profile-likelihood CIs variance components lmer / glmer fits (asymmetric, boundary-respecting; SE column, footer discloses method). re_test = "lrt" / "rlrt": opt-per-term tests variance components – boundary-corrected LR test exact restricted LRT – filling otherwise empty test columns Random effects rows. ci_method = "profile" gives profile-likelihood CIs glm, polr, clm; new ci_method = "boot_percentile" (vcov = "bootstrap") reports percentile bootstrap CIs replicates bootstrap SEs. footer names method. Heteroskedasticity- cluster-robust vcov supported classes. Resampling footers report valid replicate count, bootstrap / jackknife whose replicates nearly fail raises spicy_resampling_failed instead silently reporting classical SEs. AME columns available betareg, mgcv::gam, svyglm, survreg, fixest, estimatr, quantreg::rq, AER::ivreg, rms fits, per outcome category polr / clm / multinom. AME SEs, CIs, p-values honour robust vcov. Two-part models show full model: zero component pscl::zeroinfl / hurdle ziformula / dispformula components glmmTMB render labelled row blocks full inference (show_components = FALSE opt ). component exponentiated link yields ratio. Class-aware fit-statistics defaults: pseudo-R² ordinal multinomial fits; every class falls back nobs + AIC instead blank block. N (groups) row upgrades plain counts (e.g. N (Subject)) models share single grouping factor. Six new vignettes: Mixed-effects, Multinomial, Count two-part, Survival, Ordinal regression tables, plus Categorical predictors — cross-cutting guide dummy coding reference levels, joint tests factor, ordinal predictors (scores vs dummies), successive-difference contrasts, continuous predictors categorized.

title: spicy (development version)
text: Wide multi-model tables split stacked panels cleanly: continuation panels carry empty stub rows, -wide column spanners truncate visible ellipsis. exponentiate = TRUE visible SE column, footer states SE scale (delta method) CI bounds asymmetric. show_fit_stats = FALSE suppresses fit-statistics block. Placeholder cells decimal-align gt / flextable / tinytable / Word / Excel outputs; "deviance" prints 1 decimal; descriptive tables use single font Word outputs.

Refused clear error families. nnet::multinom fits now report McFadden’s Nagelkerke’s pseudo-R² (categorical families ). tokens silently dropped show_fit_stats , weighted fit’s null log-likelihood ignored weights. ordinal::clm(scale = ~) fits now render scale (dispersion) coefficients Scale effects block. silently absent table: estimated component model went unreported. rows stay log scale exponentiate = TRUE (exponential ratio latent standard deviations, odds ratio) footer says . broom::tidy() gains outcome_level column naming response category per-category rows (ordinal multinomial average marginal effects). rows previously indistinguishable – four age AME rows sharing one term, nothing identify category. random-effects LR test longer prints negative statistic (-0.00) singular fits: statistic clamped zero boundary p-value now 1 – half chi-bar-squared null distribution point mass zero, zero statistic carries evidence (printed 0.500, ceiling halved-chi-squared formula). ci_level now reaches random-effect variance-component CIs; three mixed engines hardcoded 95% rows. Large mixed fits longer spend minutes variance-component SEs: options("spicy.re_se_max_n") (default 1000) cells omitted, note warning giving override. nested = TRUE now works multinom (LR chi-square rows) defaults LRT rows Cox comparisons (lm’s R² / F-change rows undefined partial likelihood). AME columns silently rendered empty now populated (fixest, estimatr, quantreg, AER::ivreg, rms, pscl) refused pointer ?table_regression_models (classes AME backend). statistic column header follows model’s actual reference distribution (z t); hardcoded t. Factor coefficient AME rows follow levels() order (alphabetical); ordered factors AME columns show reference row; ame_ci / ame_p / ame_se populate without bare "ame" token; stars anchor B (AME), never beta. Bootstrap / jackknife standardized = "refit" refits longer leak caller’s environment now work factor() / log() / poly() formulas; failed refit falls back warning instead silently changing method. Standardized beta rows mixed fits inherit B’s reference distribution (showed second p-value test); standardized-coefficient table note states interaction convention fallback-aware. Titles: binomial mixed / survey fits link-aware (probit fit longer titled “Logistic”); Tobit titles name response; ordinal titles name shared-slopes assumption link; proper nouns keep capitals multi-model titles. Mixed fits cbind() responses get LR test -random-effects model; labels accepts coefficient-level keys mixed fits; polr / clm detect non-uniform prior weights. flexsurv: probit-scale splines ancillary-parameter covariates refuse exponentiate = TRUE; factor predictors group parent variable reference row. as_structured() rich output engines match console body exactly (blank vs em-dash reference cells, multi-outcome Outcome row); structured schema gains reference_models_by_row outcome_labels_by_col. table_regression(m1, m2) without list() errors helpful message; colliding model labels longer break output = "gt"; p_adjust footer’s family size matches adjustment performed; ci_method = "profile" robust vcov defers vcov warns; singular-fit note states fact leaves advice build-time warning.

title: spicy 0.12.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-19

title: spicy 0.12.0
text: New table_regression(): publication-ready coefficient summary one fitted lm glm models, side side. APA Manual 7 formatting default. Highlights: Robust variance: classical, HC, cluster-robust (CR) Satterthwaite df, bootstrap, jackknife. Per-model vcov accepted SE-comparison tables. Standardisation: refit, posthoc, basic, smart, pseudo (last glm ). Average marginal effects (AME) separate columns; AME inference shares coefficient’s variance estimator B AME reported inferential footing. Partial effect sizes: f², η², ω² lm (noncentral-F CIs); partial χ² glm. GLM response-scale reporting via exponentiate = TRUE, family-appropriate labels (, IRR, HR, RR, MR, exp(B)) optional profile-likelihood CIs (ci_method = "profile"). Multiplicity correction via p_adjust (stats::p.adjust() method). Hierarchical comparison via nested = TRUE (ΔR² / F-change lm; LRT glm). Display controls: variable filtering, intercept factor placement, reference-row styles, multi-model labels, stars, decimal mark, per-column digits. Outputs: console, data.frame, long tibble, gt, flextable, tinytable, Excel, Word, clipboard. broom::tidy() broom::glance() methods supported. See ?table_regression vignette("table-regression"). table_continuous_lm() gains additive covariate adjustment via new covariates argument. Two estimands per-group adjusted means: "proportional" (G-computation, default) "balanced" (equal-weight synthetic grid). adjustment, f² ω² become partial effect sizes; d g raise explanatory error. auto-built footer documents covariates estimand. See vignette("table-continuous-lm"). New exported as_structured() accessor returns typed view table_regression() result programmatic use: raw numerics, CI split LL / UL columns, column-level format specification.

Integer cells mixed- precision columns (n row alongside R²) keep right- aligned placement. title: spicy 0.11.0
text: Cluster-robust SEs via cluster four vcov choices ("CR0"–"CR3"), dispatched clubSandwich Satterthwaite df (clubSandwich Suggests). vcov = "bootstrap" (nonparametric cluster) vcov = "jackknife" (leave-one-/ leave-one-cluster-) variance estimators pure base R, controlled boot_n. Three new effect_size choices alongside "f2": Cohen’s "d", Hedges’ "g" (two-group ), Hays’ "omega2". New effect_size_ci adds noncentral t / F CIs rendered inline 0.18 [0.07, 0.30]. HC* estimators delegate sandwich::vcovHC(); rank-deficient fits return clean rank--rank covariance.

title: spicy 0.11.0
text: Shared reporting vocabulary (decimal_mark, p_digits, align, named-labels) now spans cross_tab(), freq() three table_*() helpers, including APA-style p-value notation (<.001 / .045, leading zero). table_categorical()’s assoc_measure accepts per-variable spec. measures differ across rows column collapses "Effect size" APA-style Note. line documents per-variable measure; phi non-2x2 errors. three table_*() functions gain .data.frame(), tibble::as_tibble(), broom::tidy() broom::glance() methods (broom Suggests).

title: spicy 0.11.0
text: Classed conditions. Errors warnings now carry stable classes (spicy_error / spicy_warning plus 11 leaf classes documented ?spicy), downstream code can dispatch via tryCatch() / withCallingHandlers() instead matching message strings. rlang (>= 1.1.0) required. Structured cli messages. Multi-line errors warnings (vcov fallbacks, bootstrap/jackknife failures, padding migration, labels length mismatch) render cli bullets. Locale-deterministic ordering. Sorts varlist(), freq(), cross_tab() table_*() use method = "radix". Output byte-stable across locales platforms, matching Stata / SPSS guarantees. Edge-case hardening. new length-guarded sort helper makes varlist() / code_book() / cross_tab() / freq() survive zero-length -NA Date / POSIXct / character columns factors observed levels. Snapshot-locked rendering. tests/testthat/test-snapshots.R pins exact console output every spicy print method, unintended formatting drift surfaces PR diff. API stability contract. ?spicy documents exports stable, stabilising internal. pkgdown reference groups exports via four @family tags. Cross-software validation. 13 association measures agree PSPP 2.0 (CROSSTABS /STATISTICS=, 65 / 65 statistics four datasets); Cohen’s d Hedges’ g noncentral CIs tested numerically effectsize::cohens_d() / effectsize::hedges_g() (tolerance = 1e-6); point-estimate formulas asymptotic standard errors follow DescTools (Signorell et al.).

title: spicy 0.1.0
text: Introduces collection tools variable inspection, descriptive summaries, data exploration. Extract variable metadata display compact summaries (varlist()). Compute frequency tables (freq()), cross-tabulations (cross_tab()), Cramer’s V categorical associations (cramer_v()). Generate descriptive statistics means (mean_n()), sums (sum_n()), counts (count_n()) automatic handling missing data. Copy data (copy_clipboard()) directly clipboard quick export.
